explanations.1: ARG declares a build-time variable that can be overridden when building the image, like passing --build-arg PHP_VERSION=8.2-apache to docker build.
explanations.3: The ${PHP_VERSION:-latest} syntax means 'use the PHP_VERSION variable, but fall back to latest if it wasn't set'.
explanations.5: set -e -x makes the script stop on any error (e) and print each command before running it (x), which helps catch and debug problems.
explanations.6: docker-php-ext-install compiles and installs PHP extensions, while docker-php-ext-enable activates them so PHP knows to use them.
